text.content: MINI PRD: P58 — Troubleshooting Software Freezing
Status: Plan
Batch: 6 | Priority: P58 | Type: Troubleshooting | Word Target: 800-1200
What This Article Does Now
Lists potential causes of FluidFlow freezing or hanging. Minimal diagnostic flow, no prioritized troubleshooting, no log file analysis. No opening statement, no internal links.
What It Should Do After Refactor

Serve as the diagnostic guide for FluidFlow freezing issues. Open with the problem (software unresponsive, calculation hangs, UI freezes), provide a prioritized troubleshooting flow (large model → calculation loop → memory limit), and resolution steps.
Specific Changes Required

**Add opening statement** (2-3 sentences): State the freezing problem (software unresponsive, calculation never finishes, UI locks up), what the reader will diagnose, and name FluidFlow directly. No preamble.

**Expand to troubleshooting flow format**: Prioritized checklist starting with most common causes (very large network → calculation not converging → insufficient RAM → corrupted model file → antivirus blocking file access).
**Add "Quick Diagnostic" section**: How to identify cause (check calculation messages for convergence warnings, monitor RAM usage in Task Manager, check file size vs. available memory).

**Add "Resolution Steps" for each cause**: Large network (simplify model, break into sub-models), non-convergence (adjust boundary conditions, check for closed loops, review P20 Troubleshooting Guide), insufficient RAM (close other apps, upgrade RAM, use 64-bit version), corrupted file (restore from backup, save as new file).
**Add "Forced Shutdown" section**: If FluidFlow truly frozen, how to safely close (Task Manager → End Task) and recover unsaved work (check auto-save location).
**Add "When to Contact Support" callout**: If freezing persists with small models or on fresh install, escalate to support@fluidflowinfo.com with model file and error logs.
**Add internal linking structure** (see below).
**Add CTA**: Contact Us (https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact) for persistent freezing issues.
**Add meta description**: 155-160 chars with primary keyword + benefit.
Glossary Links (first use only)
None applicable for troubleshooting
Internal Links
UP: P20 FluidFlow Troubleshooting Guide (general troubleshooting context)
SIDEWAYS: P56 Network License Speed Issues, P57 Restoring Missing Toolbars, P59 Network Build Error
DOWN: None (no glossary)
CTA: Contact Us — https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact
Selling Message
None (troubleshooting is support content, not a pitch moment)
Grounding Tree Branches Used
Calculation alerts: 118 design alerts, warnings, hints (convergence warnings included)
System requirements: RAM, CPU, OS (from spec sheet)
Support: support@fluidflowinfo.com, +1 888 711 3051
MINI PRD: P59 — Troubleshooting Network Build Error
Status: Plan
Batch: 6 | Priority: P59 | Type: Troubleshooting | Word Target: 800-1200
What This Article Does Now

Lists potential causes of "Network Build Error" and "Unable to Prepare Network" messages. Minimal diagnostic flow, no specific error code interpretation, no resolution validation. No opening statement, no internal links.
What It Should Do After Refactor

Serve as the diagnostic guide for network build failures. Open with the problem (can't calculate model, "Network Build Error" message, "Unable to Prepare Network"), provide a prioritized troubleshooting flow (disconnected pipes → missing boundary → conflicting inputs), and resolution validation.
Specific Changes Required

**Add opening statement** (2-3 sentences): State the build error problem ("Network Build Error" on calculate, "Unable to Prepare Network", calculation won't start), what the reader will diagnose, and name FluidFlow directly. No preamble.

**Expand to troubleshooting flow format**: Prioritized checklist starting with most common causes (disconnected pipes or junctions → missing boundary condition → conflicting flow/pressure specs → component data incomplete → closed loop without flow boundary).

**Add "Quick Diagnostic" section**: How to identify cause (check Messages list for red error icons, visually inspect for disconnected components, verify at least one flow or pressure boundary exists).

**Add "Resolution Steps" for each cause**: Disconnected components (use Snap Grid, zoom in to check connections), missing boundary (add pressure or flow boundary at system inlet/outlet), conflicting specs (remove duplicate boundaries, check for over-constrained system), incomplete data (fill in required fields in Data Palette, check for red highlights).
**Add "Validation" section**: After fixing, how to confirm network builds successfully (Messages list shows green "Network built successfully", calculation proceeds).
**Add "When to Contact Support" callout**: If error persists on simple models or with all data entered, escalate to support@fluidflowinfo.com with model file and error messages.
**Add internal linking structure** (see below).
**Add CTA**: Contact Us (https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact) for persistent build errors.
**Add meta description**: 155-160 chars with primary keyword + benefit.
Glossary Links (first use only)
None applicable for troubleshooting
Internal Links
UP: P20 FluidFlow Troubleshooting Guide (general troubleshooting context)
SIDEWAYS: P07 How to Build a Model, P08 How to Enter Input Data, P09 How to Calculate a Model, P58 Troubleshooting Software Freezing
DOWN (component glossary): P83 Hydraulic Components - Boundaries
CTA: Contact Us — https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact
Selling Message
None (troubleshooting is support content, not a pitch moment)
Grounding Tree Branches Used
Boundaries: Pressure, Flow, Tank/Vessel Reservoir, Atmospheric Ends (4 types)
Calculation alerts: 118 design alerts, warnings, hints (includes build errors)
Messages list: Red error icons, green success messages
Support: support@fluidflowinfo.com, +1 888 711 3051
MINI PRD: P60 — FluidFlow Comprehensive FAQ
Status: Plan
Batch: 6 | Priority: P60 | Type: FAQ | Word Target: 2000-3000
What This Article Does Now
Lists common questions about FluidFlow. Minimal depth in answers, no categorization, no internal linking to deep-dive articles. No opening statement, no glossary links.
What It Should Do After Refactor

Serve as the central FAQ hub for FluidFlow — the page that answers 80% of pre-sales and early-user questions. Open with the FAQ purpose (quick answers to common questions, links to deep-dive articles), organize by category (Getting Started, Licensing, Calculation Capabilities, Troubleshooting, Data Management), and link extensively to relevant KB articles.
Specific Changes Required

**Add opening statement** (2-3 sentences): State the FAQ purpose (quick answers to most common questions, starting point for deeper exploration), what the reader will find, and name FluidFlow directly. No preamble.

**Expand to FAQ depth** (~2,000 words): Organize questions into 5-7 categories (Getting Started, Licensing & Installation, Calculation Capabilities, Modules & Correlations, Data Management, Troubleshooting, Training & Support).

**Each answer should**: Provide 2-4 sentence summary, then link to relevant deep-dive article for full details (e.g., "Q: How do I size a pump? A: FluidFlow's Auto-Size Pump feature calculates required head and flow based on system demand. See P23 Auto-Size Pump Feature for step-by-step guide.").
**Add "Top 10 Questions" section at top**: Most frequently asked questions with jump links to answers below.
**Add "Getting Started" category**: Installation, activation, first model, UI overview, system requirements.
**Add "Licensing & Installation" category**: License types, transfer, updates, network vs. standalone, trial access.

**Add "Calculation Capabilities" category**: What systems can FluidFlow model? (liquid, gas, two-phase, slurry, non-Newtonian), What correlations are available? (link to P01 Product Overview, P13 QA & Standards), Can it do transient? (No — coming in FlowDesigner), Can it model heat transfer? (Yes — all modules).

**Add "Modules & Correlations" category**: Which module for my application? (link to P01 Product Overview), What's the difference between two-phase correlations? (link to spec sheet or future article), What equations of state are available? (Peng-Robinson, Lee-Kesler, BWRHS — NOT SRK).

**Add "Data Management" category**: How to add custom fluids, pipes, fittings? (link to P43 Database, P70-P82 database addition articles), How to export reports? (link to P25 Export Reports), How to save scenarios? (Multi-Calc feature — link to P22).
**Add "Troubleshooting" category**: Model won't calculate (link to P59), Software freezes (link to P58), Toolbars missing (link to P57), Convergence issues (link to P20).

**Add "Training & Support" category**: Where to find tutorials? (link to P93 Video Library, P94 Webinars, P95 Free Training), How to contact support? (support@fluidflowinfo.com, +1 888 711 3051), How to verify results? (link to P55 Results Verification).
**Add internal linking structure** (see below).
**Add glossary links on first use** (see below).
**Add CTA**: Free Trial (https://fluidflowinfo.com/freetrial/) for hands-on answers, Contact Us (https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact) for questions not covered.
**Add meta description**: 155-160 chars with primary keyword + benefit.
Glossary Links (first use only)
[Duty Point](https://fluidflowinfo.com/glossary/#duty-point) — in pump sizing question
[NPSH](https://fluidflowinfo.com/glossary/#npsh) — in pump troubleshooting question
[System Curve](https://fluidflowinfo.com/glossary/#system-curve) — in system analysis question
[Deposition Velocity](https://fluidflowinfo.com/glossary/#deposition-velocity) — in slurry question
Internal Links
UP: None (FAQ is a hub, not a child)
SIDEWAYS: P01 Product Overview, P13 QA & Standards, P20 Troubleshooting Guide, P43 Database, P55 Results Verification, P93 Video Library, P94 Webinars, P95 Free Training
DOWN: Links to ALL relevant articles (P05, P06, P07, P08, P09, P16, P22, P23, P25, P51, P52, P53, P56, P57, P58, P59, P70-P82, P83-P91 component glossary)
CTA: Free Trial — https://fluidflowinfo.com/freetrial/, Contact Us — https://fluidflowinfo.com/contact
Selling Message

7 — "Short learning curve with comprehensive support" — weave into the Training & Support section naturally: FluidFlow's tutorials, webinars, and support team help new users become productive quickly. Do not repeat as a separate pitch.
Grounding Tree Branches Used
All 5 modules: Liquid, Gas, Two-Phase, Slurry, Scripting
All correlations: 4 liquid, 8 two-phase, 8 slurry, 4 rheology, 3 EOS
All standards: API RP 520, ISO 4126-1, ISO 5167, ANSI/ISA-75.01.01
All auto-sizing: Pumps, Relief, Fans, Orifice, Control Valves
Database: 1,283 fluids, 14 pipe types, 5 custom fluid types
Support: support@fluidflowinfo.com, +1 888 711 3051, live chat, email
Training: Video library, webinars, free basic training
MINI PRD: P61 — Connecting Pipes
Status: Plan
Batch: 7 | Priority: P61 | Type: How-to | Word Target: 800-1200
What This Article Does Now
Basic instructions for connecting pipes in FluidFlow. Minimal context on connection modes, snap behavior, or troubleshooting misalignments. No opening statement.
What It Should Do After Refactor

Quick how-to guide for pipe connections. Open with the task (connecting pipes to junctions, extending networks), provide clear steps, explain snap grid behavior, and troubleshoot common connection issues.
Specific Changes Required
**Add opening statement** (2-3 sentences): State the task (connecting pipes to build networks), what the reader will learn, and name FluidFlow directly.
**Expand to step-by-step format**: Numbered list (select pipe tool → click start point → click end point → verify connection).
**Add "Snap Grid Behavior" section**: How snap grid helps alignment, how to toggle (link to P64 Snap Grid).
**Add "Troubleshooting Connections" section**: Common issues (pipes not connecting, junctions appear disconnected, gap between pipe and junction).
**Add internal links**: P62 Placing Component, P64 Snap Grid, P67 Reposition Nodes.
**Add CTA**: Free Trial (https://fluidflowinfo.com/freetrial/).
Glossary Links (first use only)
None
Internal Links
UP: P07 How to Build a Model
SIDEWAYS: P62 Placing Component, P64 Snap Grid, P67 Reposition Nodes
DOWN: None
Selling Message
None
Grounding Tree Branches Used
Pipes: Standard, Rectangular/Annulus, Hose (from spec sheet)

**Add "Component Palette Organization" section**: How components are grouped (Boundaries, Boosters, Valves, etc.) — link to component glossary (P83-P91).
**Add "Troubleshooting Placement" section**: Component won't place, component appears but can't connect, wrong component type.

**Add "Undo/Redo" section**: How to undo accidental deletion (Ctrl+Z / Cmd+Z).

**Expand to step-by-step format**: Numbered list (select component → drag resize handles → confirm).
**Add "Visual Only" callout**: Resizing components does NOT change hydraulic calculations — it's purely for visual presentation.
**Add "When to Resize" section**: Large flowsheets, presentations, highlighting critical equipment.
**Add internal links**: P67 Reposition Nodes, P69 Rotating Components.
**Add CTA**: Free Trial (https://fluidflowinfo.com/freetrial/).
